steps.title: The Unmasking
steps.text: A discovered attack happens when you move one of your pieces and reveal an attack from another piece behind it. When the revealed attack is a check AND the moved piece also checks, it's called a double check — the most devastating tactic in chess.

steps.prompt: Move the knight to expose the rook — better yet, have the knight ALSO attack the king.
steps.hint: Nd6 attacks e8 AND unveils the rook. Double check!

steps.title: Why It's Lethal
steps.text: Against a double check, the king MUST move — you can't block two attacks with one piece, and capturing one leaves the other. Double checks are among the fastest mating patterns in chess.
